accept: act skill file is in version control somewhere — either committed in the act repo (with a copy/symlink mechanism to ~/.claude/skills/act/) or embedded in the binary.
Skill updates are diffable across versions and recoverable from history.
A user who installed act via brew/curl (no act repo clone) can still get the skill installed alongside the binary.
Documented in act help and in the bootstrap path (act-6051) so users know how to install/update the skill.
payload.description:
The act skill at ~/.claude/skills/act/SKILL.md is the canonical workflow doc for every project using act. Today it's not in git — ~/.claude is not itself a repo. Means: skill edits are unrecovered if corrupted, can't be diffed across changes, can't be shared between machines without manual copy.

Cleanest fix: keep the skill file inside the act repo (e.g. act/skills/act-skill/SKILL.md) and have a small bootstrap script or symlink chain that points ~/.claude/skills/act/ at the act repo's copy. Then skill updates ride act's release cadence and are visible in act's git log.

Tradeoff: ties skill availability to having the act repo cloned somewhere. For users who just installed act via brew/curl, the skill needs to come along. Could be solved by: (a) packaging the skill as part of the binary release, (b) a tiny 'act install-skill' subcommand that copies the embedded skill file out, (c) embedding the skill file's contents into the binary itself via go:embed.

Pick the simplest path that works for both 'I cloned the act repo' and 'I brew-installed' use cases.
payload.title: version-control the act skill file (untracked at ~/.claude/skills/act/)
